source_jobs.title: Sales Representative (Closer)
source_jobs.description:
Meet ACQ:

At Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com, we’re a team of entrepreneurs on a mission to make real business education available to everyone. There are 34 million small and medium businesses in the United States. They’re the backbone of the economy, but many will never reach their full potential because the education available to them is outdated, impractical, and built for classrooms instead of companies.

We’re fixing that by giving founders and business owners the tools, knowledge, and support they need to grow. The proof is in our results:

 - Built an Advisory Practice that’s already served more than 3,500 clients in its first year

 - Broke the world record for non-fiction book sales in 24 hours with $100M Money Models https://www.amazon.com/100M-Money-Models-Make-Acquisition-com-ebook/dp/B0FMXTZ4MH

 - Scaled to nine figures in 18 months without any outside capital

We believe in high standards, hard work, and helping others win. If that resonates, you’ll feel at home here.




SALES TEAM VISION:

 - We are building a leadership factory. Meaning - we are looking for reps to come in and prove themselves, develop skills, and move fast in their career.

 - We aim to promote only from within. We want to build you not only into a world-class closer, but a future leader.

 - For this reason, every sales rep we hire first proves themselves as an SDR (setter), then is eligible for promotion to a closer. If you cannot set, you cannot close.

 - You’ll be responsible for selling our scaling workshop tickets ($5,000) to our prospective clients. You will be our first point of contact maintaining and growing the http://acquisition.comAcquisition.com http://Acquisition.com brand.




WHO THIS IS FOR:

 1. You are a top performing rep at your company

 2. You have tried a business and it didn’t work out

 3. You are extremely hard working

 4. You are looking for growth

 5. You have business acumen (plus if you’ve sold to small business owners).




WHAT WE OFFER YOU:

 - Daily Sales Training & 1-1 coaching

 - Professional Skill Development (leadership, business strategy, marketing, etc.)

 - Performance based career growth (the best get promoted)

 - Uncapped commissions




WHAT WE EXPECT FROM YOU:

Responsibilities:

 - High work ethic (Monday-Saturday work schedule)

 - Follow our proven sales process

 - Train with your coach daily

 - Manage your pipeline & follow up properly

 - Admin work for data collection

 - Be a team player

Results:

 - Minimum of 60 tickets sold per month

 - Minimum of 45% close rate

Requirements:

 - 1-2 years of experience in transactional sales (preferred high transactional sales experience to SMBs)

 - Self-sufficient and able to properly manage one’s own time

 - Open to learning new processes in sales

 - Exceptional over the phone

 - Excellent communication skills
   
   


LOCATION:

 - Remote, USA (option to re-locate for top performer)

 - Acquisition.com is authorized to hire in the following states:
   
   - AL, AZ, CO, FL, GA, IL, IN, KS, LA, MD, MI, MN, MO, NC, NE, NJ, NV, OH, OR, PA, SC, TN, TX, UT, WA, WI
     
      


COMPENSATION:

 - $65k Base Salary + Uncapped Commission

 - OTE $150k-$200k+
   
   


BENEFITS:

We offer a comprehensive, evolving benefits package designed to support your health, family, and wellbeing. Some key offerings:

 - Flexible Unlimited Paid Time Off and Company-wide Holidays

 - Employer sponsored Medical, Dental, & Vision plans
   
   - $1,950 annual Employer HSA contribution
   
   - FSA options including dependent care
   
   - Employee assistance program and mental health resources

 - Employer match program for 401(k), eligible for both Traditional and Roth accounts

 - $1,200 annual wellness reimbursement through JOON https://joon.io/ that supports health, family care, pet care, fitness, and more!

 - For local or visiting team members, enjoy access to a state of the art gym at our HQ in Las Vegas

* Benefits eligibility applies only to

source_jobs.title: Youtube Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ACQ MEDIA

ACQ Media is the in-house media engine behind Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com and its portfolio of brands led by Alex Hormozi, Leila Hormozi, and Sharran Srivatsaa.

Our mission is to build the leading business media organization in the world by 2030.

We produce high-performance long-form series, podcasts, and daily social content that reach millions of people around the globe. Our content is not vanity media; it is designed to drive measurable reach, deepen brand influence, and create downstream business impact across the entire Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com ecosystem.

We operate like a studio, not a startup content team. That means structured workflows, batch production, clear performance metrics, and an aggressive commitment to innovation, especially through AI-native production systems.

We are building a repeatable content machine that compounds: more output, higher quality, faster iteration, stronger monetization, and deeper brand equity over time.

If you're joining ACQ Media, you're not here to just make content. You're here to help build a media powerhouse.


ROLE

The YouTube Manager exists to turn Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com's business priorities into long-form videos that perform at their ceiling and drive measurable business outcomes across the ACQ Media ecosystem.

This is a true channel-owner seat across all Acquisition brands (Alex, Leila, and Sharran). You have meaningful input upstream on ideas, angles, series concepts, and formats, informed by performance data and a deep read of what the YouTube audience responds to. You help shape what gets made and how it gets recorded, so YouTube performance is built in from the start, not bolted on at the end.

This is not a channel babysitter or a packaging-only seat. You own titles, thumbnails, metadata, upload operations, analytics, and back-catalogue optimization, and you are accountable for business outcomes: subscriber growth, qualified traffic and leads to ACQ Media properties, and YouTube's direct and downstream revenue contribution to the flywheel. Views and CTR are inputs, not the goal. The role is AI-forward by default: analytics, reporting, A/B tracking, and optimization research run on AI agents and automation, not manual spreadsheets and gut instinct.

Reports directly to the Director of Post Production and works hand in hand with the Design department and Creative Producers to shape brand identity, content production, and distribution.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the creative direction of YouTube packaging (titles and thumbnails) across all ACQ brands, partnering with Creative Producers and Design to translate each brief into the final thumbnail.

 - Run the full upload operation end to end across all channels, including scheduling, tags, chapters, end screens, and a final QC check, so no video goes live without passing a complete checklist.

 - Build and run a continuous A/B testing framework for thumbnails and titles across new and catalogue videos, keeping every packaging decision defensible against performance benchmarks.

 - Own YouTube analytics across all ACQ channels and build AI agent-powered workflows for data compilation, anomaly detection, and reporting, then translate insight into creative, writing, and production decisions.

 - Systematically audit and re-optimize the back catalogue (thumbnails, titles, descriptions, metadata) on a prioritization framework, and track the compounding impression and revenue lift over time.

 - Serve as the primary liaison to YouTube's Partner Manager and lead the evaluation and rollout of new platform features against a clear test-and-measure approach.


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years managing, growing, or optimizing a high-volume YouTube channel.

 - Deep command of YouTube's packaging ecosystem: thumbnails, titles, CTR mechanics, retention patterns, and algorithm behavior.

 - Strong analytical fundamentals, comfortable drawing decisions from data, with demonstrated A/B

source_jobs.title: Social Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ACQ MEDIA

ACQ Media is the in-house media engine behind Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com and its portfolio of brands led by Alex Hormozi, Leila Hormozi, and Sharran Srivatsaa.

Our mission is to build the leading business media organization in the world by 2030.

We produce high-performance long-form series, podcasts, and daily social content that reach millions of people around the globe. Our content is not vanity media; it is designed to drive measurable reach, deepen brand influence, and create downstream business impact across the entire Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com ecosystem.

We operate like a studio, not a startup content team. That means structured workflows, batch production, clear performance metrics, and an aggressive commitment to innovation, especially through AI-native production systems.

We are building a repeatable content machine that compounds: more output, higher quality, faster iteration, stronger monetization, and deeper brand equity over time.

If you're joining ACQ Media, you're not here to just make content. You're here to help build a media powerhouse.


ROLE:

The social coordinator is the tactical engine of the media department. While the social producer defines the "what" and the "why," you own the "how" and the "when." You will turn the execution layer that turns great strategy into flawless, on-time content. While we value creative eyes, this is not a strategy or directing position; it is a role for an operational specialist who takes pride in being the backbone of flawless social channels.

This is an execution role. You are responsible for the last mile of production: ensuring every piece of content is perfectly packaged, captioned, and published on time across all platforms. You have full tactical ownership of our social channels, acting as the final gatekeeper before content reaches our viewers.

This role requires extreme discipline and a deep, native understanding of platform tools. You are the person who ensures nothing falls through the cracks, from file naming conventions to community engagement.

This role reports directly to the Social Media Producer and must be in Las Vegas, NV or willing to relocate.


RESPONSIBILITIES:


PLATFORM MANAGEMENT & PUBLISHING

 - Manage the daily publishing calendar and lead the end-to-end upload process for Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Threads, and any future platforms we pursue.

 - Navigate platform-native tools (YT Studio, TikTok Studio, etc.) to optimize metadata, tags, and thumbnails for maximum reach

 - Maintain a 100% on-time posting rate, ensuring the brand remains consistent and reliable in the eyes of the algorithm


COPYWRITING & PACKAGING

 - Draft high-performance captions and headlines at the direction of the social media producer and in partnership with the team writer

 - Adapt copy for platform-specific constraints (e.g., character limits vs. long-form captions)

 - Ensure every post has a clear, functional CTA that aligns with the broader brand goals, including tools like ManyChat etc)


COMMUNITY MANAGEMENT & TREND SPOTTING

 - Monitor comments and engage with the community to foster brand loyalty and sentiment

 - Flag emerging audio, formats, or cultural trends to the social media producer for content opportunities

 - Provide the social media producer with weekly checks on how the audience is responding to specific content pieces


ASSET & WORKFLOW MANAGEMENT

 - Ensure all social assets are properly labeled, stored, and easily retrievable within our internal systems

 - Manage any tools and tracking links to ensure the downstream business impact is measurable

 - Assist the social media producer during shoots with asset capture, BTS footage, and organizational support


REQUIREMENTS:

 - 1+ years of experience in social media coordination, digital marketing, or junior production

 - Expert-level knowledge of TikTok, Instagram, YouTube Shorts, Facebook, and LinkedIn native managem

source_jobs.title: Senior AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ROLE

The Senior AI Engineer builds and deploys production-grade AI agents that power ACQ Vantage. This role exists to turn AI capability into real, usable systems that drive business outcomes, not experiments or prototypes.

You will work inside a lean Technology team to design, build, and ship agentic workflows that interact with internal tools, data systems, and user-facing products. You are responsible for taking ideas from concept to production, with a focus on reliability, speed, and practical value.

This is a hands-on engineering role. You are writing code daily, iterating quickly, and working directly with modern AI tooling. You are expected to understand how LLMs behave in production, not just how they work in theory.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, build, and deploy production-grade AI agents and end-to-end agentic workflows that solve real business problems across ACQ Vantage

 - Integrate LLMs with internal systems, APIs, and data sources, ensuring reliability, performance, and clean abstractions

 - Collaborate with product and engineering teams to prioritize, ship, and iterate on AI features quickly

 - Own and improve RAG pipelines across multiple Pinecone namespaces, including chunking strategy, embedding model selection, hybrid retrieval, and reranking

 - Build and maintain an evaluation framework, including golden datasets, automated quality scoring, retrieval metrics, latency benchmarks, and regression detection

 - Optimize model routing and tiering to improve unit economics while maintaining output quality

 - Instrument the AI layer for observability, including cost-per-request, token usage, quality signals, and anomaly detection


REQUIREMENTS

 - 7+ years shipping production software systems (distributed backends, APIs, deployment pipelines, monitoring)

 - 2+ years building production RAG systems using vector databases (Pinecone, Qdrant, FAISS, or Weaviate), including embedding strategies, index management, and retrieval tuning

 - Built and deployed AI agents or multi-step LLM workflows in production, including tool use, orchestration, and system integrations

 - Built or contributed to an evaluation framework for an LLM-based product (retrieval quality measurement, regression detection, model-switching decisions based on data)

 - Reduced LLM API costs in production through model routing, caching, token management, or architectural improvements

 - Worked across multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, or equivalent) and understands tradeoffs in prompt behavior, token economics, and failure modes

 - Comfortable in both TypeScript and Python (our stack uses both)


RESULTS

 - Production AI agents are deployed and actively used within ACQ Vantage

 - New AI-driven features move from concept to production in weeks, not months

 - Agent performance improves over time through structured testing and iteration

 - AI systems operate reliably with minimal failure or manual intervention

 - Engineering output translates directly into measurable business impact


SCHEDULE

 - Work hours aligned with local timezone or Pacific timezone (8a-5p M-F)

 - Availability to support critical launches or events when needed


LOCATION

 - Full-time, remote (US-based)

 - Quarterly on-sites in Las Vegas


COMPENSATION:

 - $170,000 - $200,000 base salary

 - The salary range may be inclusive of several levels that would be applicable to the position. Final salary will be based on a number of factors including, level, relevant prior experience, skills, and expertise. This range is only inclusive of base salary, not benefits (more details below).


BENEFITS:

We offer a comprehensive, evolving benefits package designed to support your health, family, and wellbeing. Some key offerings:

 - Flexible Unlimited Paid Time Off and Company-wide Holidays

 - Employer sponsored Medical, Dental, & Vision plans
   
   - $1,950 annual Employer HSA contribution
   
   - FSA options including dependent care
   
   - Employee assistan

COMMISSION STRUCTURE:

source_jobs.title: Social Media Producer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ACQ MEDIA

ACQ Media is the in-house media engine behind Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com and its portfolio of brands led by Alex Hormozi, Leila Hormozi, and Sharran Srivatsaa.

Our mission is to build the leading business media organization in the world by 2030.

We produce high-performance long-form series, podcasts, and daily social content that reach millions of people around the globe. Our content is not vanity media, it is designed to drive measurable reach, deepen brand influence, and create downstream business impact across the entire Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com ecosystem.

We operate like a studio, not a startup content team. That means structured workflows, batch production, clear performance metrics, and an aggressive commitment to innovation, especially through AI-native production systems.

We are building a repeatable content machine that compounds: more output, higher quality, faster iteration, stronger monetization, and deeper brand equity over time.

If you’re joining ACQ Media, you’re not here to just make content. You’re here to help build a media powerhouse.


ROLE:

We’re hiring a Social Media Producer to lead the growth and performance of the social ecosystem behind ACQ Media and the personal brands of Alex Hormozi, Leila Hormozi, and Sharran Srivatsaa. This role is for someone who has scaled meaningful accounts and can point to measurable results.

ACQ Media is the in-house media engine producing long-form series, podcasts, and daily short-form content that reaches millions globally. Our media is engineered to drive measurable brand growth and downstream business impact, not vanity engagement. We operate like a studio with structured workflows, batch production, performance metrics, and AI-native systems.

In this role, you will own the strategy, editorial direction, and performance of all short-form and platform-native content. You will turn long-form episodes into high-retention, high-distribution assets that drive traffic back to YouTube and strengthen the overall brand ecosystem. You are the bridge between production and distribution, responsible for ensuring speed, cohesion, and measurable impact.

This is a management role based in Las Vegas. You will directly manage the Social Coordinator, own the publishing pipeline, and build repeatable systems that compound over time. We are looking for a growth-minded operator who understands platforms deeply, actively integrates AI into workflows, and treats social as a performance channel.


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Own the end-to-end social growth strategy across all platforms, translating long-form content into high-performing short-form assets that drive traffic, retention, and measurable brand growth.

 - Lead and develop the Social Coordinator, setting strategic direction while ensuring disciplined execution of the publishing calendar and platform distribution.

 - Identify high-retention moments within long-form content and provide clear editorial direction to editors to maximize performance and cohesion.

 - Build and optimize repeatable production systems, including AI-driven workflows that increase speed, output, and quality.

 - Monitor performance data across platforms, translate insights into actionable pivots, and continuously improve strategy based on measurable results.

 - Partner with the production team to ensure social-first capture, organized asset management, and rapid turnaround from long-form production to distribution.


REQUIREMENTS:

 - 5+ years of experience in social production, digital strategy, or video-first content growth, with a proven track record of scaling accounts through original video content.

 - Demonstrated experience managing at least one direct report and owning performance outcomes.

 - Strong editorial judgment. You can consistently identify high-retention moments within long-form content and understand why they will perform on specific platforms.

 - Deep platform fluency across YouTube, Inst

source_jobs.title: Graphic Designer
source_jobs.description:
The Graphic Designer is responsible for creating high-quality visual design across ACQ Media and the broader Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com ecosystem.



This role supports everything from premium media brands and YouTube content to internal initiatives, marketing campaigns, company presentations, websites, events, and new business ventures. You’ll work across multiple brands, departments, and formats, helping ensure every visual touchpoint reflects a consistent standard of excellence.



Responsibilities:

* Designing brand identities and visual systems for shows, podcasts, business units, and company initiatives

* Creating graphics for YouTube, social media, websites, presentations, and marketing campaigns

* Supporting internal teams with creative assets that elevate communication and execution across the organization

* Building scalable templates, design systems, and asset libraries that increase consistency and production speed

* Leveraging AI to accelerate creative workflows without sacrificing quality



Results:

* Every ACQ Media brand has a cohesive, recognizable visual identity

* Thumbnail quality consistently improves content performance

* Brand systems reduce production time while increasing consistency

* Creative assets are delivered on time with minimal revisions

* AI workflows materially improve design output and team efficiency

* Internal stakeholders can quickly access templates and design assets without reinventing work

* ACQ Media becomes recognized for world-class visual design across every platform



Requirements:

* 4+ years of professional graphic design experience in media, agency, or content production

* Outstanding portfolio demonstrating strong branding, digital design, and marketing creative

* Expert proficiency in the Adobe Creative Suite and Figma

* Experience designing for YouTube, social media, and digital marketing

* Experience building scalable brand systems rather than isolated assets

* Familiarity with AI creative tools such as Claude, Gemini, Firefly, Higgsfield,or equivalent

* Ability to manage multiple concurrent projects while maintaining exceptional quality

* Strong communication skills and comfort receiving direct creative feedback




You are not simply designing assets. You are helping define the visual language of one of the fastest-growing business media companies in the world.



This role requires exceptional taste, versatility, and execution. You’ll move seamlessly between brand identity work, marketing design, content graphics, presentation design, and digital creative while maintaining an uncompromising standard of quality.



This role reports directly to the Director of Art & Design.


BENEFITS:

We offer a comprehensive, evolving benefits package designed to support your health, family, and wellbeing. Some key offerings:

 - Flexible Unlimited Paid Time Off and Company-wide Holidays

 - Employer sponsored Medical, Dental, & Vision plans
   
   - $1,950 annual Employer HSA contribution
   
   - FSA options including dependent care
   
   - Employee assistance program and mental health resources

 - Employer match program for 401(k), eligible for both Traditional and Roth accounts

 - $1,200 annual wellness reimbursement through JOON https://joon.io/ that supports health, family care, pet care, fitness, and more!

 - For local or visiting team members, enjoy access to a state of the art gym at our HQ in Las Vegas

* Benefits eligibility applies only to full-time exempt roles.




ACQ CORE VALUES:

Our core values are the heart and soul of this incredible company. The right person for this role will appreciate each of these values, personally subscribe to them, and understand why each is critical to having a great business.


COMPETITIVE GREATNESS

Be at your best when your best is needed. Enjoyment of a hard challenge. Those who have the drive to constantly improve, the superior intellect and long term commitment to see incremental improvements becom

source_jobs.title: Director of Product
source_jobs.description:
ROLE

The Director of Product exists to decide what we build next and ensure it drives real results for our customers.

Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com has strong distribution and access to customers. The challenge is not demand. The challenge is making better product decisions. This role turns customer behavior, support signals, and usage data into clear product direction across multiple product areas.

This is a hands-on, founding product role. You are not managing a mature PM team or maintaining roadmaps as artifacts. You are making decisions. You define what products should exist, how they evolve, and where we place bets. You use AI as a core tool to prototype, test, and accelerate iteration.

You report to the CTO and partner closely with Operating Company Presidents. They bring deep knowledge of their customers. You translate that into product direction that improves retention, engagement, and outcomes.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Define product direction across the portfolio by turning customer support patterns, usage data, and business context into clear product bets

 - Analyze how customers actually use the product and advocate for the end user in product decisions

 - Identify whether product efforts should be unified, separated, or repositioned based on real usage and market signals

 - Partner with Operating Company Presidents to translate customer insight into product priorities that drive retention and engagement

 - Use AI tools to rapidly prototype, test, and refine product ideas before committing engineering resources

 - Stay close to execution by shaping requirements, making trade-offs with engineering, and ensuring decisions result in shipped product


REQUIREMENTS

 - Proven ability to turn ambiguous inputs into product decisions that lead to shipped products and measurable business outcomes

 - Experience building or scaling products in 0 to 1 or early-stage environments, including operating without defined structure

 - Built or stood up a product function where one did not previously exist, not inherited a team or maintained an existing roadmap

 - Managed a multi-product portfolio simultaneously, making prioritization decisions across competing products

 - Partnered directly with business operators or revenue owners to translate customer insight into product direction

 - Made build vs. buy decisions with real budget consequences and can clearly explain the reasoning behind at least one


RESULTS

 - Clear product direction is established across the portfolio, with aligned understanding across leadership

 - Measurable improvement in retention or engagement driven by product decisions

 - Product decisions reflect real customer behavior, not assumptions or internal bias

 - AI is actively used to accelerate prototyping, testing, and iteration speed

 - At least one major product decision materially improves how customers experience or use the product

 - Leadership can clearly articulate what is being built and why, based on your direction


SCHEDULE

 - Standard business hours with flexibility based on collaboration with engineering and leadership


LOCATION

 - Full-time, remote (US-based)

 - Quarterly on-sites in Las Vegas


COMPENSATION:

 - $170,000 - $200,000 base salary

 - The salary range may be inclusive of several levels that would be applicable to the position. Final salary will be based on a number of factors including, level, relevant prior experience, skills, and expertise. This range is only inclusive of base salary, not benefits (more details below).


BENEFITS:

We offer a comprehensive, evolving benefits package designed to support your health, family, and wellbeing. Some key offerings:

 - Flexible Unlimited Paid Time Off and Company-wide Holidays

 - Employer sponsored Medical, Dental, & Vision plans
   
   - $1,950 annual Employer HSA contribution
   
   - FSA options including dependent care
   
   - Employee assistance program and mental health resources

 - Employer match

source_jobs.title: Video Editor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ACQ MEDIA

ACQ Media is the in-house media engine behind Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com and its portfolio of brands led by Alex Hormozi, Leila Hormozi, and Sharran Srivatsaa.

Our mission is to build the leading business media organization in the world by 2030.

We produce high-performance long-form series, podcasts, and daily social content that reach millions of people around the globe. Our content is not vanity media, it is designed to drive measurable reach, deepen brand influence, and create downstream business impact across the entire Acquisition.com http://Acquisition.com ecosystem.

We operate like a studio, not a startup content team. That means structured workflows, batch production, clear performance metrics, and an aggressive commitment to innovation, especially through AI-native production systems.

We are building a repeatable content machine that compounds: more output, higher quality, faster iteration, stronger monetization, and deeper brand equity over time.

If you’re joining ACQ Media, you’re not here to just make content. You’re here to help build a media powerhouse.

Responsibilities

Testimonial & Workshop Pipeline (Primary Lane)

 - Own the end-to-end post-production workflow for all Advisory Practice testimonial footage and workshop (L1, L3) captures.

 - Turn raw workshop footage into polished testimonial ads, VSLs, and marketing assets at high volume and consistent quality.

 - Intake, organize, and triage incoming footage against established ad formats and marketing priorities.

 - Maintain a clean backlog of ready-to-publish assets so the AP marketing team always has creative assets to deploy.

AI-Augmented Editing Workflows

 - Operate inside the automated AI short-form pipeline being built by the Media team — this is not optional, it's how throughput gets hit.

 - Use AI tools for rough cuts, transcription, sound cleanup, captioning, and clip repurposing as a core part of daily workflow, not a supplement.

 - Contribute feedback to improve the AI pipeline as you use it; flag what's working and what needs refinement.

 - Consistently apply AI-assisted workflows to compress edit cycles without degrading quality or compliance standards.

Craft & Quality

 - Edit to direct-response standards: hooks, pacing, emotional arc, and clear CTAs that drive program consideration.

 - Apply ACQ Media file naming conventions, server workflows, and asset management standards from day one.

 - Maintain FTC-compliant disclaimer standards on all testimonial content per internal compliance guidelines.

 - Collaborate with the Advisory Practice marketing team on creative direction, ad testing priorities, and revision feedback.

Throughput & Reliability

 - Hit weekly output targets for ad volume — this is a production role and velocity matters.

 - Communicate blockers early and keep project boards current so nothing falls through.

 - Flex to Media team content projects when AP workload dips.

 
 

Requirements

 - 3+ years of professional video editing experience, with a portfolio that includes direct-response or performance marketing content (ads, VSLs, testimonials strongly preferred).

 - Demonstrated experience with AI-assisted editing tools and automated workflows — you need to have actually used these, not just be "open to learning."

 - Strong proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro; familiarity with After Effects a plus.

 - Experience editing testimonial, interview, or event footage at volume.

 - Working knowledge of direct-response ad structure: hooks, benefit stacking, social proof framing, CTAs.

 - Comfort operating inside structured project management systems (Monday.com http://Monday.com or similar).

 - Alignment with ACQ Media's naming conventions, server workflows, and production standards (covered in onboarding — willingness to learn and follow them rigorously is the requirement).

 
 

Results

 - Testimonial and workshop footage is turned into publish-ready ads on a consistent weekly cad

source_jobs.title: Production Manager
source_jobs.description:
ROLE:

We’re hiring a Production Manager to build and lead the production infrastructure behind ACQ Media and the personal brands of Alex Hormozi, Leila Hormozi, and Sharran Srivatsaa. This role is for someone who has managed full production pipelines at scale and can point to predictable, studio-level execution across multiple concurrent projects.

ACQ Media is the in-house media engine producing long-form series, podcasts, and daily short-form content that reaches millions globally. Our media is engineered to drive measurable brand growth and business impact. We operate like a studio with structured workflows, repeatable systems, performance visibility, and AI-native production infrastructure.

In this role, you will own the operational backbone of our media department across pre-production, filming, and post-production handoffs, often managing two to three production batches simultaneously. You will oversee casting workflows, run-of-show development, vendor coordination, crew logistics, budgeting, asset integrity, and cross-functional communication while ensuring execution remains predictable as output scales.

This is a management role based in Las Vegas. We are looking for a proactive, systems-driven operator who does not need to be taught how sets run or how production timelines are structured. You actively leverage project management software and AI tools to increase visibility, coordination speed, and operational precision.


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Own end-to-end production flow across multiple concurrent batches, ensuring predictable execution from casting and pre-production through filming and post handoff.

 - Build and manage detailed production timelines, run-of-show documents, call sheets, vendor coordination, crew logistics, travel, locations, and contingency plans.

 - Lead operational oversight on shoot days, maintaining schedule discipline, communication clarity, and studio-level standards under pressure.

 - Oversee budgeting, cost tracking, and vendor management while identifying efficiencies without sacrificing quality.

 - Enforce clean asset management, file structures, and version control to ensure zero lost footage and frictionless post-production workflows.

 - Implement and optimize project management software and AI-driven systems to increase visibility, coordination speed, and operational precision.


REQUIREMENTS:

 - 5+ years of experience managing production flow in a professional studio, agency, broadcast, digital-first, or news-style production environment.

 - Demonstrated experience overseeing multiple concurrent production batches with complex, multi-step processes.

 - Strong experience with project management software such as Monday, Asana, or similar platforms, with the ability to build structured production systems.

 - Proven ownership of budgets, vendor coordination, crew logistics, and run-of-show documentation.

 - Systems-oriented, proactive operator who leads confidently in high-pressure environments and actively integrates AI tools to improve operational efficiency.


RESULTS:

 - Production pipelines run smoothly across multiple concurrent batches without missed steps or preventable delays.

 - Shoot days operate on schedule and at consistent studio quality with clear run-of-show execution.

 - Zero lost footage with organized, searchable asset archives and clean post-production handoffs.

 - Teams operate with clarity and confidence, knowing what to do and when to do it.

 - Operational systems scale to support increased output without increased chaos.


LOCATION:

 - Las Vegas, NV (3 days onsite per week)

 - Onsite attendance is required for all on-set filming days

 - Support for relocation to Las Vegas will be provided

 - Light travel may be required


COMPENSATION:

 - $80,000 - $90,000 base salary

 - The salary range may be inclusive of several levels that would be applicable to the position. Final salary will be based on a number of factors including, level, relevant pr

notes: Mostly sales and media roles, with a few tech and product positions.
